planning_gate.spec_delta: M19 now evaluates depth schedules around exp86's recoverable 3-phase controller before moving to learned finetuning.

perspectives.product: tests whether the same G1 can reach the visible squat gate by schedule search before adding another training loop
perspectives.architecture: keeps exp86's 3-phase controller and adds full-rollout optimizer scoring over schedule variants
perspectives.security: no credentials or external side effects
perspectives.qa: native sweep records raw JSON, visible gate metrics, and optimizer score per schedule
perspectives.skeptic: a small schedule grid may only reconfirm the local 5cm recoverable optimum

dod: raw native JSON per schedule variant
summary ranks schedules and states whether any variant passes visible_8cm_gate

sources.note: G1 lower-body degrees of freedom, joint range, and knee torque make squat geometry plausible.
ASAP reports agile Unitree G1 whole-body skills including squat and squat plus lean, but uses learned motion tracking and sim-to-real alignment.
Whole-body control work motivates unified locomotion/control rather than raw pose tracking for fine-grained humanoid motion.
